title: Student loan repayment and default
description: Practice understanding of deferment, forbearance, default.

assessment_items.question_content:
If you are struggling to make your student loan payments, you may ask your loan servicer to enter into a deferment.

**What is the primary benefit of loan deferment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It lowers the interest rate on existing loans.
It temporarily suspends your loan payments.
It reduces the total amount you owe.
It eliminates all future payments.

assessment_items.question_content:
If you are struggling to make your student loan payments, you may be eligible for loan deferment, if you meet certain qualifications.

**Which of the following could qualify you for loan deferment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: experiencing unemployment or economic hardship
maintaining a high credit score
enrolling in school at least half-time
being self-employed

assessment_items.question_content:
While you are in college, your student loans are deferred. After you graduate, you may defer the loans again if you are struggling to make payments.

**Which of the following are a potential consequence of placing your student loans in deferment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your total loan cost increases due to accrued interest.
Deferment extends the time it takes to pay off your loan.
Your loan is due in its entirety at the end of deferment.
Your credit score significantly decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
If you are struggling to make your student loan payments, you may ask your loan servicer to enter into a forbearance.

**What is the primary purpose of forbearance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to eliminate a portion of your student loan debt
to temporarily pause or reduce monthly loan payments
to decrease the interest rate on the loan
to forgive the remaining balance after $10$ years

assessment_items.question_content:
Let’s assume you place your federal student loan in forbearance.

**What happens to the amount you owe after the forbearance period ends?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You owe the same amount as before forbearance.
You owe less than before forbearance.
You owe more than before forbearance.
You owe nothing after forbearance.

assessment_items.question_content:
If you are struggling to make your student loan payments, you may be eligible for loan forbearance, if you meet certain qualifications.

**Which of the following could qualify you for loan forbearance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: experiencing temporary financial difficulties
maintaining a high credit score
facing unexpected medical expenses
starting a new business

assessment_items.question_content:
Defaulting on a student loan can lead to some serious consequences. 

**What are some potential consequences of defaulting on your student loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: collection fees and legal action
bankruptcy
wage garnishment
increase in interest rates on other debts

assessment_items.question_content:
Being late on student loan payments causes the loan to progress through different stages.

**Arrange the following options to show the stages a student loan goes through, starting with missing a payment.**

[[☃ sorter 4]]
hints: Loan is **delinquent** when not paid by due date.
After $90$ days it is **reported late** to credit bureaus.
After $270$ days of missed payments, a student loan enters **default** status.
Starting with a missed payment, a student loan will first be  $\boxed{\text{delinquent}}$ > $\boxed{\text{reported late}}$ > $\boxed{\text{default}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Defaulting on a student loan can lead to some serious consequences. 

**How long does it take for a student loan to go into default?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: as soon as a payment is late
$90$ days
$270$ days
$10$ years

assessment_items.question_content:
US Department of Education tracks the default rate by each school (cohort).

In 2019, Radford University in Virginia had a default rate of $1.8$.

**What does this rate tell us about Radford University's graduates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A default rate is given as a decimal but actually represents a *percentage* of students who are in default. The "$\%$"is not shown but it is implied.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
A default rate of $1.8$ means that $1.8\%$ of borrowers who are currently repaying their loans are actually in default.
$1.8\%$ out of $100\%$ represents a small portion. Schools with a default rate of $20\%$ (or higher) risk closure by the Department of Education.
Radford University's low default rate tells us that **only a small percentage of borrowers are in default**.

choices.content: A majority of graduates are in default.
A majority of graduates got good paying jobs.
Not many students qualified for financial aid.
Only a small percentage of borrowers are in default.

assessment_items.question_content:
US Department of Education tracks the default rate by each school (cohort).

In 2019, Bluefield University in Virginia had a default rate of $3.4$.

**What does this rate tell us about Bluefield University's graduates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A default rate is given as a decimal but actually represents a *percentage* of students who are in default. The "$\%$"is not shown but it is implied.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
A default rate of $3.4$ means that $3.4\%$ of borrowers who are currently repaying their loans are actually in default.
$3.4\%$ out of $100\%$ represents a small portion. Schools with a default rate of $20\%$ (or higher) risk closure by the Department of Education.
Bluefield University's low default rate tells us that **only a small percentage of borrowers are in default**.

choices.content: A majority of graduates are in default.
A majority of graduates got good paying jobs.
Not many students qualified for financial aid.
Only a small percentage of borrowers are in default.

assessment_items.question_content:
US Department of Education tracks the default rate by each school (cohort).

In 2018, the student loan default rate at Everest College in Chesapeake, Virginia was reported to be $36$.

**What does this rate tell us about Everett College's graduates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A default rate is given as a decimal but actually represents a *percentage* of students who are in default. The "$\%$"is not shown but it is implied.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
A default rate of $36$ means that $36\%$ of borrowers who are currently repaying their loans are actually in default.
$36\%$ out of $100\%$ represents a large portion. Schools with a default rate of $20\%$ (or higher) risk closure by the Department of Education.
Everett College's high default rate tells us that **a large percentage of borrowers are in default**.

choices.content: A small portion of graduates are in default.
A majority of graduates got low-paying jobs.
Not many students qualified for financial aid.
A large percentage of borrowers are in default.
